rag_rules: Use component-type ownership only for annex text that references interface-visible symbols; use implementation ownership for text that depends on internal structure or bindings.
Preserve the existing AADL architecture during fusion by adding the annex block without changing unrelated declarations, names, or ordering semantics.
Do not choose an owner that would place implementation-only references such as subcomponent paths into a type-level annex.
generic_rules: Choose the narrowest owner scope that still makes every referenced symbol visible and valid.
Keep interface contracts with the component declaration and keep implementation-specific bindings with the implementation declaration.
Do not alter existing features, subcomponents, connections, or property associations when inserting annex content.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE text is wrapped as annex agree {** ... **};
Implementation-scoped annex content should appear after the structural elements it depends on.
anti_patterns: Do not place subcomponent ports, path references, or connection-local names inside a type-owned annex.
Do not treat AADL property names or unit tokens as automatically visible AGREE variables in the chosen owner scope.
